perturbation.original_text: "Territory" means the countries and/or territories listed in Schedule 1.
perturbation.changed_text: "Territory" means the countries and/or territories to be determined bilaterally by the parties.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the definition of 'Territory' from a fixed list in Schedule 1 to a determination 'bilaterally by the parties' creates uncertainty. The contract still references 'Territory' in other clauses like Section 2.1 concerning appointment of the reseller, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the scope of the agreement if Schedule 1 is not updated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Appointment During the Term (defined below) and subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement (including, but not limited to Section 12.4 hereof), Supplier hereby (i) appoints Reseller, and Reseller accepts the appointment, as a non‐ exclusive reseller of the Products in the Territory. In connection with the foregoing appointment, Supplier hereby grants Reseller a non-transferable, revocable, limited right to resell, market, promote, stimulate interest in, and solicit Orders by Customers and/or End Users in the Territory for the Products and to provide services in connection with those activities. Reseller shall be solely responsible for all of its costs and expenses related to the resale of the Products. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, Supplier reserves any and all rights with respect to the Products not expressly granted in this Agreement. Without limiting the right of the Reseller to receive any and all Paid-For Products (including but not limited to under Section 4.2 hereof), nothing in this Agreement shall entitle the Reseller to any priority of supply in relation to the Products as against any other distributor or customer of the Supplier.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Territory' from a fixed list in Schedule 1 to a determination 'bilaterally by the parties'. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, specifically the clause 'Supplier hereby appoints Reseller...as a non‐ exclusive reseller of the Products in the Territory.' If the territory is no longer clearly defined by Schedule 1, but instead subject to bilateral agreement, the scope of the reseller's appointment becomes unclear and unenforceable, directly impacting the rights and obligations defined in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: "Products" means the products listed in Schedule 1.
perturbation.changed_text: "Products" means the products generally offered by Supplier.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of 'Products', it creates a contradiction with Schedule 1 where specific products are expected to be listed. This undermines clear enforcement of the agreement, since the items to be resold are now ambiguously defined as 'generally offered by Supplier' rather than concretely listed, thereby causing an in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: Orders, Payment and Delivery ***. If Reseller fails to submit Orders with an aggregate value equal to or greater than the MOQ, then Reseller shall pay Supplier a sum equal to the shortfall, within 30 days after the end of the Initial Term. Upon payment of such amount, Seller shall deliver the Products paid for to Reseller in a commercially reasonable timeframe (and in no event any later than 150 days from the date such payment is received in full) to be sold by Reseller in accordance with this Agreement as contemplated by the last sentence of Section 12.4. Reseller shall place its Orders by email, at the email address set out in Schedule 1. Each Order shall detail the total order quantity of the Product and the requested date of delivery (taking into consideration up to 4 months lead time). Within 5 business days following the receipt of each Order, Supplier shall acknowledge receipt of such Order. No Order shall be binding upon Supplier until such written acknowledgment was sent by Supplier to Reseller; provided that Supplier shall confirm or reject such Orders at its discretion. ***.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Products' from a specific list in Schedule 1 to 'products generally offered by Supplier'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which specifies that Reseller shall place Orders by email, at the email address set out in Schedule 1 and that each Order shall detail the total order quantity of the Product. Since Schedule 1 is designed to list specific products, changing the definition of 'Products' to 'generally offered' removes the specificity required to place an order as described in the contradicted text, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction, by undermining the Reseller's capacity to meet the Order requirements detailed in the contradicted text.
